title: scandix pecten-veneris f. pinnatifida
intro: Scandix pecten-veneris f. pinnatifida is a subspecies of the Scandix pecten-veneris plant, commonly known as the Shepherd's Needle or Shepherd's Needle Wild Cress, belonging to the family Apiaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Scandix pecten-veneris f. pinnatifida include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reduction of fever and pain. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and dried powders, which can be used to make teas, capsules, or added to topical creams.
